name: classical_velocity_additive
statement: theorem classical_velocity_additive (u v : ℝ) : u + v = v + u
description: Galilean velocity addition: any real velocities add commutatively — no universal speed limit

name: lorentz_invariant_real
statement: theorem lorentz_invariant_real (v c : ℝ) (hc : c > 0) (hv_sq_lt : v^2 < c^2) : 0 < 1 - v^2 / c^2
proof:
have h_sq : c^2 > 0 := pow_pos hc 2
  have h_div : v^2 / c^2 < 1 := (div_lt_one h_sq).mpr hv_sq_lt
  linarith
description: Lorentz factor is real and positive — c bounds all velocities, fundamental to special relativity

name: classical_energy_dense
statement: theorem classical_energy_dense (E : ℝ) : ∃ E' : ℝ, E' ≠ E
description: Energy spectrum is a continuum — for any energy value, there exists another distinct value arbitrarily close

name: planck_energy_discrete_step
statement: theorem planck_energy_discrete_step (n : ℤ) (ℏω : ℝ) (hℏω : ℏω > 0) : (n : ℝ) * ℏω ≠ (n : ℝ) * ℏω + ℏω / 2
proof:
intro h_eq
  have h_half : ℏω / 2 = 0 := by linarith
  have h_zero : ℏω = 0 := by linarith
  linarith
description: Planck energy quantization — energy at nℏω cannot be at a half-step between integer multiples; discrete spectrum

name: classical_momentum_proportional
statement: theorem classical_momentum_proportional (m v : ℝ) : m * v = v * m
description: Newtonian momentum p=mv — continuous real-valued, commutative in mass and velocity

name: de_broglie_momentum_relation
statement: theorem de_broglie_momentum_relation (p l h : ℝ) (hl : l ≠ 0) (h_rel : p * l = h) : p = h / l
description: De Broglie relation p = h/λ — momentum is wave-like, wavelength is fundamental; quantum wave-particle duality

name: euclidean_distance_nonneg
statement: theorem euclidean_distance_nonneg (x y z : ℝ) : x^2 + y^2 + z^2 ≥ 0
description: Euclidean distance squared is non-negative definite — absolute space, positive definite metric

name: minkowski_interval_indefinite
statement: theorem minkowski_interval_indefinite (c : ℝ) : ∃ (t x : ℝ), c^2 * t^2 - x^2 < 0
description: Minkowski interval can be spacelike (negative) — indefinite metric, fundamental to special relativity spacetime structure

name: classical_time_difference_invariant
statement: theorem classical_time_difference_invariant (t1 t2 : ℝ) : t1 - t2 = -(t2 - t1)
proof:
calc
    t1 - t2 = -(t2 - t1) := by ring
description: Time intervals are frame-invariant — Galilean absolute time, same for all observers

name: relativistic_simultaneity_offset
statement: theorem relativistic_simultaneity_offset (Δx v c : ℝ) (hc : c > 0) (hΔx : Δx ≠ 0) (hv : v ≠ 0) : v * Δx / c^2 ≠ 0
proof:
apply div_ne_zero
  · exact mul_ne_zero hv hΔx
  · exact pow_ne_zero 2 hc.ne.symm
description: Relativity of simultaneity — time offset vΔx/c² between frames is non-zero; events simultaneous in one frame are not in another

name: classical_mass_additive
statement: theorem classical_mass_additive (m1 m2 : ℝ) : m1 + m2 = m2 + m1
description: Newtonian mass is additive and conserved — total mass is sum of parts, independent of velocity

name: relativistic_energy_momentum_bound
statement: theorem relativistic_energy_momentum_bound (E p m c : ℝ) (hc : c > 0) (hm : m ≥ 0) (h_rel : E^2 = (p*c)^2 + (m*c^2)^2) : E^2 ≥ (m*c^2)^2
description: Einstein energy-momentum relation E² = (pc)² + (mc²)² — energy is bounded below by rest mass energy; mass-energy equivalence

name: classical_light_speed_unbounded
statement: theorem classical_light_speed_unbounded (c : ℝ) : ∃ v : ℝ, v > c
description: No universal speed limit — for any speed c, there exists a faster speed; Maxwell's c has no special status

name: photon_energy_quantized
statement: theorem photon_energy_quantized (E ν h : ℝ) (hh : h > 0) (h_rel : E = h * ν) : E / h = ν
proof:
calc
    E / h = (h * ν) / h := by rw [h_rel]
    _ = ν := by field_simp [hh.ne.symm]
description: Planck-Einstein relation E = hν — light energy is quantized, carried by discrete photons; E/h yields frequency

name: classical_spin_continuous
statement: theorem classical_spin_continuous (s : ℝ) : ∃ s' : ℝ, s' ≠ s
description: Angular momentum / spin is a continuous real variable — any real value is possible classically

name: quantum_spin_half_integer_spectrum
statement: theorem quantum_spin_half_integer_spectrum (n m : ℤ) : ((n : ℝ) / 2) - ((m : ℝ) / 2) = (((n - m : ℤ) : ℝ) / 2)
description: Spin quantum number takes half-integer values — difference between any two spin states is also a half-integer; discrete spectrum

name: classical_position_momentum_commute
statement: theorem classical_position_momentum_commute (x p : ℝ) : x * p = p * x
description: Position and momentum are commuting observables — they can be simultaneously measured with arbitrary precision

name: quantum_uncertainty_product
statement: theorem quantum_uncertainty_product (Δx Δp ℏ : ℝ) (hℏ : ℏ > 0) (h_ineq : Δx * Δp ≥ ℏ / 2) : Δx * Δp > 0
description: Heisenberg uncertainty principle: Δx·Δp ≥ ℏ/2 > 0, so neither uncertainty can vanish — exact simultaneous measurement is impossible

name: classical_field_superposition
statement: theorem classical_field_superposition (f g : ℝ → ℝ) (x : ℝ) (a b : ℝ) : (a • f + b • g) x = a * f x + b * g x
description: Classical fields superpose linearly — field values are real-valued functions, superposition yields exact deterministic values

name: quantum_vacuum_energy
statement: theorem quantum_vacuum_energy (ℏ ω : ℝ) (hℏ : ℏ > 0) (hω : ω > 0) : ℏ * ω / 2 > 0
description: Quantum field vacuum energy ℏω/2 is strictly positive — zero-point energy; classical vacuum has zero energy, quantum vacuum does not

name: newton_gravity_inverse_square_symmetric
statement: theorem newton_gravity_inverse_square_symmetric (G m1 m2 r : ℝ) (hr : r ≠ 0) : G * m1 * m2 / r^2 = G * m2 * m1 / r^2
description: Newtonian gravity is symmetric in mass — force is continuous, scale-free, with no built-in length cutoff

name: planck_length_finite
statement: theorem planck_length_finite (G ℏ c : ℝ) (hG : G > 0) (hℏ : ℏ > 0) (hc : c > 0) : Real.sqrt (ℏ * G / c^3) > 0
description: Planck length ℓ_P = √(ℏG/c³) is strictly positive and finite — quantum gravity introduces a minimal length scale; spacetime is not infinitely divisible
